Motivation
Operations around mem_state are moved to debug mode to reduce workload on critical path for hierarchical caching.

sglang.title: Fix RotaryEmbedding when using Triton backend for EXAONE-3.5-2.4B
sglang.author: lkm2835
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

https://huggingface.co/LGAI-EXAONE/EXAONE-3.5-2.4B-Instruct
EXAONE-3.5-2.4B-Instruct used head_dim 80.
--attention-backend flashinfer does not support head dim 80.
Then, I use --attention-backend triton to avoid flashinfer.
python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path /model/EXAONE-3.5-2

sglang.title: Unify variable naming: replace is_in_free_group with is_not_in_free_group
sglang.author: c1lovez1
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Fix the variable name inconsistency in the TokenToKVPoolAllocator and PagedTokenToKVPoolAllocator classes.
In the clear method, the incorrect variable name self.is_in_free_group was used, while self.is_not_in_free_group is used in other methods of the classes. This PR unifies the variable name to se

sglang.title: [ROCm] Enable MTP (NextN) on AMD GPU
sglang.author: alexsun07
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
To enable MTP (NextN) on AMD GPU.
Modifications
Added a few kernels to torch_extension_rocm.cc. Enabled MTP test for AMD GPU.
Benchmark result
# benchmark
python3 -m sglang.bench_one_batch_server --model None --base-url http://127.0.0.1:30000 --batch-size 1 --input-len 256 --output-len 25

sglang.title: [Quant Kernel] refactored per token group quant fp8 to support int8 up-to 2x faster
sglang.author: zcnrex
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
#2965
The new cuda kernel accelerates 1x-2x compared to triton kernel. Refactored the fp8 kernel so it works for both fp8 and int8 output quantization types.
✅ torch.int8 implementations match
✅ torch.float8_e4m3fn implementations match
per-token-group-quant-8bit-performance:
    batch_si

vllm.title: [Misc] Add tuned R1 w8a8 and MoE configs for NVIDIA L20
vllm.author: DefTruth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Reopen #15117 and #15126 as one completed PR here.
Hi~, we are the tech team from vipshop.com and mainly use vLLM in our production deployment. This PR is mainly focused on optimizing DeepSeek-R1 671B inference performance on NVIDIA L20 devices using vLLM with pp3 + tp8. We hope that this PR will fu

vllm.title: [Bugfix] fix torch.compiled cache hash error
vllm.author: DefTruth
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Without this fix, different values of VLLM_PP_LAYER_PARTITION from the environment will generate the same cache hash. However, different values of VLLM_PP_LAYER_PARTITION will affect the generation of the torch.compiled graph. Therefore, setting VLLM_PP_LAYER_PARTITION to a value different from the

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode] Respect prompt_lookup_max
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Currently, V1's N-gram spec decoding ignores prompt_lookup_max and only uses prompt_lookup_min. This PR fixes this issue.

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode] Use better defaults for N-gram
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Current Issues

prompt_lookup_max parameter is mandatory in all cases
prompt_lookup_min defaults to 1, which is too small for most use cases (too many matches)

Changes Implemented

When only one lookup parameter is provided, the other will automatically match it
When neither parameter is specified,

vllm.title: [Frontend] Support tool calling and reasoning parser
vllm.author: WangErXiao
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Remove the mutual exclusion restriction between tool calling and reasoning parser, as models like QwQ-32B can support both functionalities simultaneously. Additionally, having tool calling parse only from the content rather than the reasoning_content can improve the accuracy and performance of tool

vllm.title: [Misc][Doc] Add note regarding loading `generation_config` by default
vllm.author: ywang96
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#12622 introduced a change to apply generation_config, therefore can sometimes silently override default sampling parameters and cause user confusion. See #15241 as an example.
This PR updates the documentation regarding this change and gives a warning if any of the sampling parameters is overridden

vllm.title: [V1] Enable V1 Fp8 cache for FA3 in the oracle
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Now that FA3 Fp8 support has landed (#14570) we can enable Fp8 KV-caches for Hopper devices in V1
Update the oracle to allow this plus basic refactors
Test script

# Sample prompts.
prompts = [

vllm.title: [Fix] [torch.compile] Improve UUID system for custom passes
vllm.author: ProExpertProg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Removed pickling for UUID used for caching in 2.6 and maintained backwards compatibility with pickling in 2.5.
FIX #15223
Addresses cruft from #12393

vllm.title: Fix non-contiguous input passed to Marlin kernel
vllm.author: Qubitium
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
With vllm main + DeekSeek R1 GPTQModel quantized model + Marlin + Triton MLA V1 + kv/prefill caching == Crash due to x (input) not contiguous.
Core cause: The kv/prefill caching is passing non-contiguous x (input) to lower kernels and some (Marlin) kernels only operate on contiguous memory layout.
R
